steps.title: Identify the grammatical subject and its number
steps.content: The grammatical subject is “analysis” (singular). “James Melton and Tom Ginsburg's” is a possessive phrase modifying “analysis”; it does not make the subject plural.

steps.title: Determine subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
A singular subject requires a singular verb. “Identifies” matches this requirement with its singular simple present form.

The other three choices—”identify,” “have identified,” and “are identifying”—are all plural forms that violate the agreement rule.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
